title: Analogy Test (Type 3)
blurb: Find what is common across a set of terms.

theory.intro: There are certain items associated with one another in different ways. We have to show our best alternate choice for one, which is more reasonable and an appropriate. For example:

options: They are Greek mythological gods
They are stars
They are planets
They are signs of Zodiac
examples.explanation: The above all the four solutions are related with the problem but alternate (d) is the most appropriate. Thus, (d) is the our best choice.

questions.question: Academy, University, College?
options: They mean the same thing
They deal with education of the students
They are all institutions
They are common places of worships

questions.question: Monkey, Ape, Chimpanzee?
options: They are herbivorous animals
They are vertebrates
They are primates
They all live on trees

questions.question: House, Apartment, Bungalow?
options: These are all made of bricks and mortar
They are found in cities
They are dwelling places
These are the names of residences

questions.question: Fingerprints, Investigation, Post-mortem?
options: They are terms connected with medical science
They are in alphabetic order
They are technical terms generally used for detecting crimes
It is a process used by crime-branch

questions.question: Thallophyta, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta?
options: This is a classification of flowerless plants
These are botanical terms
These are the names of flowers
These are terms used in Botany

questions.question: Reticule, Hand-bag, Valise?
options: They are synonyms
They are used for carrying small personal belongings
They are used by women only
They are the part of modern life

questions.question: Petal, Stamens, Stigma?
options: They are the part of a flower
They are the part of a plant
They are concerned with pollination of flower
They are different kinds of leaves

questions.question: Falsehood, Misrepresentation, Deception?
options: They are synonyms
They are all ungentlemanly acts
They are the modern way of expression
They are mostly used during elections

questions.question: Goat, Cow, Buffalo?
options: They are all animals
They are found all over the world
They all give milk
They all have red, brown colour

questions.question: Seminar, Symposium, Workshop?
options: They are various types of conferences of specialists for discussion
These activities have the same purpose that of solving a problem
These are various methods in which people meet
These are various methods adopted for teaching a group

questions.question: Turnip, Knol-kohl, Cabbage?
options: They are all botanical things
They are all flowers
They are all vegetables
They are all common weeds

questions.question: Carbohydrates, Proteins, Fats?
options: They are compounds of carbon
They are essential ingredients of a good diet
They are complex compounds
They are found in plants

questions.question: Glucose, Fructose, Sucrose?
options: They are important sugars
They are found in animal tissues
They are organic compounds
They are synthesised from Carbon and Hydrogen
